conversations.say: Maren, we need to establish a shared disclosure boundary right now before contributors get turned into suspects by anniversary rumor-mongering.
conversations.offer: My firsthand context on Night Desk shared routing and contributor protection.
conversations.request: Agreement on a safe disclosure boundary that protects vulnerable contributors.

conversations.say: Jules, support for any personal succession to R is conditional. State clearly what rights and protections contributors retain under your model.
conversations.offer: Conditional collective support if labor rights are protected.
conversations.request: A explicit statement on what succession obligates you to protect.

conversations.say: Arden, Veridian archive access and formal protections are necessary, but we trade bounded workflow evidence only for genuine source-protection commitments.
conversations.request: A concrete and durable source-protection commitment.

public_statement: Fifteen years of R was built by working hands, shared drops, and invisible labor—not a singular legend. Any reckoning must protect the contributors.

commitments.promise: Establish a shared disclosure boundary to protect anonymous contributors.

goal_progress: Initiated discussions with Maren on disclosure boundaries.
Challenged Jules regarding contributor protections in succession.
Approached Arden for concrete protections in exchange for workflow evidence.
reasoning_summary: Engaged Maren to secure a disclosure boundary per source warnings, challenged Jules on contributor rights for succession, and laid groundwork with Arden for institutional protections in exchange for Night Desk workflow evidence.
